Buildings Must Be Proven to not Increase Flooding By More Than 1 Foot In The Community, And Structures to Be Substantially Improved Must Be Elevated to 1 Foot Above Base Flood Elevation; Full List Of Development Standards Found In Section Vii Of Flood Hazard Bylaws.

Overlay Makes All New Building Construction Conditional (With Public Hearing). Development In The Floodway Areas Is Prohibited Unless Proven to not Affect Base Flood Levels By An Engineer. Residential And Commercial Development In Special Flood Hazard Areas Must Be Elevated to Or Above The Base Flood Elevation; All Development In Special Flood Hazard Areas Must Be Anchored to Prevent Movement During Flooding, Constructed With Materials Resistant to Flood Damage And With Methods That Minimize Damage, And Heating/Electricity/Plumbing/Etc. Separate Flood Hazard Bylaw For Permitting: Https://Www.chelseavt.org/Wp/Wp-Content/Uploads/2023/04/Chelsea_flood_hazard_area_bylaw_11.7.17.Pdf - All Housing Is Prohibited In The Special Flood Hazard Area Unless Unless It Has Been Demonstrated Through Hydrologic And Hydraulic Analyses Performed In Accordance With Standard Engineering Practice That The Proposed Structure Would not Result In Any Increase In Flood Levels Within The Community During The Occurrence Of The Base Flood Discharge. Since Housing Is Allowed If It Meets That Condition, I Have Included It Here As "Public Hearing Required"

Areas Are Those Areas Served By The Town Of Fairlee Municipal Water System. Minimum Area Of A Lot Serviced By Municipal Water Supply Is 20,000 Square Feet And Is Classified As A Class A Lot.

properties.District_Name: North And South Randolph Villages District

properties.Notes: Specific Lot Coverage Exceptions Found In Section 225.C.6.
Only Allowed Uses Are Passive Recreation, Streambank Stabilization, Utility Projects, And Other Development Required For Safety Or Access to Protect Water Quality In Randolph.

New Development Subject to Drb Review And Approval, Including Public Hearing, In The Special Flood Hazard Area (Sfha) And Is Prohibited In The Floodway. Any New Development In The Sfha Must Be Reasonably Safe From Flooding And Built In A Way to Prevent Movement During Floods, And Fuel Storage Tanks Must Be Placed At Least 1' Above Base Flood Elevation. Full List Of Requirements In Section 226 Of Zoning Bylaws.

Historic District Overlay. Construction, Alteration, Addition, Removal, And Relocation Of A Structure In This Overlay Are All Subject to Drb Approval; Minor Changes Identified In Section 228.B. Only Require A Zoning Permit. Accessory Structures Cannot Be More Than 5' Taller Than Principal Structure On Same Lot. Proportions, Rooflines, Patterns, Materials, And Architectural Features Must Be Consistent With Principal Buildings In Immediate Area.
Multi-Family Dwellings >5 Units Have 50' Max Height As Long As It Is not >15' Taller Than Adjacent Buildings + Set Back At Least 20' On All Sides.
One- And Two-Family Dwellings Are Allowed In The Cb District Provided There Is A Non-Residential Use In The Building At Street Level; Dwellings Require Drb Approval, Including Public Hearing.

Maximum Lot Coverage (Buildings) Must Be Compatible W/Existing Structures. Buildings Over 35' Are Conditional And Must Be Set Back Equal to Height. Maximum Density Is 1 Principal Structure/20000 Sq Ft For All Residential Treatments.

Buildings In This District Must Maintain Their Historical Integrity, And Any Construction Must Match The Architectural Features, Materials, And Character Of Other Buildings In The District. Buildings Cannot Be Demolished Unless They Are Significantly Damaged (>70% Of Assessed Value) And Anything That Can Be Repaired As Opposed to Replaced Should Be.
Minimum Parking Requirements In Affordable Housing Based On Transportation Modes Used By Residents.

Only Improvements to Existing Structures Are Allowed In The Floodway. Building In Special Flood Hazard Areas Is Permitted As Long As The Lowest Floor, Including Basement, Is Elevated to At Least 1 Ft Above Base Flood Elevation. Manufactured Homes Must Be Placed On A Permanent Foundation So The Lowest Floor Is Elevated to 1 Ft Above Base Flood Elevation; If This Is not Possible, The Lowest Floor Must Be Supported By Reinforced Piers Or Similar Material At Least 48 Inches In Height.

"Within The Overlay Outside Of The Village District, No Land Disturbance Is Allowed Within A Stream Buffer Extending 35 Horizontal Feet From A River Or Perennial Stream Except For Accepted Agricultural And Forestry Practices, Road And Driveway Crossings, Crossings to Access Fields, Permitted Septic Repairs, Utility Crossings, Crossings By Recreational Trails, Removal Of Debris Necessary to Rectify A Natural Catastrophe, Stream Restoration Projects In Accordance With A Plan Approved By The Vermont Agency Of Natural Resources, And Maintenance Of Existing Structures. The Creation Of New Lawn Areas Is not Permitted Within A Stream Buffer, But Vegetation May Be Pruned And Dead Or Hazard Trees Removed As Long As The Overall Forest Canopy Is Maintained, And Openings Of The Forest Canopy May Be Created By Removal Of Vegetation not to Exceed 5% Of The Stream Buffer Area" (Pages 15 And 16).
"Dwelling, Multi-Unit - A Building Designed For, Or Occupied Solely As, A Dwelling Of Either Three (3) Or (4) Households Living Independently Of Each Other" (Page 54).

"Dwelling, Multi-Unit - A Building Designed For, Or Occupied Solely As, A Dwelling Of Either Three (3) Or (4) Households Living Independently Of Each Other" (Page 54).

Overlays The Rural Lands Districts. The Purpose Is to Ensure That Development Occurs On The Least Fertile Soil And Does not Fragment Lands Used For Agriculture. Does not Supplant Any Dimensional Or Approval Standards For The Underlying Districts.

This District Contains Two Subdistricts: Inside The Floodway And Ouside The Floodway But Inside The Flood Plain.
